metadata.notes: Tier 2 audit: axe-core 4.10.2 injection + Playwright snapshot + manual reflow/contrast/keyboard checks. This is a private brainstorming artifact; legal exposure only relevant if shared/published.

findings.title: 23 paradigm cards are mouse-only (div with click handler, no keyboard support)
findings.affected_users: Keyboard-only users, screen reader users, motor-impaired users using switch devices
findings.description: Cards are <div> elements with click handlers but no role, tabindex, or key event listener. Tab cannot reach them, Enter/Space cannot activate. The primary atlas browsing interaction is unreachable without a mouse.
findings.fix: Make cards proper <button> elements (preferred) OR add role="button", tabindex="0", and a keydown handler for Enter/Space. Native buttons inherit focus, keyboard activation, and screen reader semantics for free.
findings.legal_exposure: WCAG 2.1.1 is a Level A requirement. In US ADA Title III lawsuits 2023-2025, keyboard inoperability is one of the top-3 cited violations.
findings.code_before:
card.addEventListener('click', () => openDetail(p.id));
// card has no tabindex, no role, no keydown

findings.title: 3 preset combo cards have click handler but no keyboard support
findings.description: The recommended combo cards (一鍵載入) cannot be activated without a mouse. Same pattern as f1.
findings.fix: Same as f1 — convert to <button> or add tabindex/role/keydown.
findings.legal_exposure: Level A violation. Same risk profile as f1.

findings.title: Synergy items in detail panel are clickable but not keyboard-reachable
findings.description: The synergy list inside detail panel lets you jump to another paradigm. Mouse-only.
findings.fix: Convert to <button> or add tabindex/role/keydown. Since synergies are inside lists, keep <li> wrapper but use a <button> inside.

findings.title: Workbench suggestion items have click handler but no keyboard support
findings.description: Same div-as-button anti-pattern. Suggestions are also draggable but drag is mouse-only too.
findings.fix: Convert to <button> or add tabindex/role/keydown handler.

findings.title: --ink-faint (#8A8578) fails AA: 3.44:1 contrast on paper background
findings.affected_users: Low-vision users, elderly users, users in bright daylight
findings.location: :root --ink-faint var, used in .filter-label, .shortlist-info, .card-meta-row, .card-cta, .head-right, .anti-list, footnote text, ~30+ usages
findings.description: axe-core flagged 66 nodes; ink-faint is the primary culprit. Used widely for small labels (10-11px). Ratio is 3.44:1 vs 4.5:1 AA minimum.
findings.fix: Darken --ink-faint from #8A8578 to #6B6655 (raises contrast to 5.4:1, still keeps muted feel).
findings.legal_exposure: WCAG 1.4.3 is the most-litigated criterion (~80% of US ADA web cases cite it).

findings.title: --cat-1 (#B85C3D) fails AA when used as small text: 4.23:1
findings.description: Need 4.5:1 for normal text. Currently 4.23. Borderline but fails.
findings.fix: Darken to #A04F33 (raises to ~4.8:1) or limit usage to ≥18pt large text where 3:1 is acceptable.

findings.title: --cat-2 (#C19A3D) catastrophically fails AA: 2.47:1 on paper
findings.location: .cat-num for cat-2 (Curriculum 課程型), border-color usages
findings.description: Used as text color for category 2 number labels. 2.47:1 ratio. Light ochre on cream is one of the worst common combinations.
findings.fix: Darken cat-2 to #8A6E1F (≈4.7:1 on paper). Will shift the gold toward dark mustard but still distinct from other cats. OR keep for borders only (3:1 is acceptable for non-text UI components) and use a darker variant for text.
findings.code_after:
--cat-2: #8A6E1F;  /* darker ochre, 4.7:1 */
--cat-2-border: #C19A3D;  /* keep light for borders/accents */

findings.title: Page overflows horizontally at 320px viewport (WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow)
findings.affected_users: Mobile users, zoom users (zoom equivalent of 400% on 1280px desktop)
findings.location: main { max-width: 1640px; padding: 0 64px } and .cards { grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr)) }
findings.description: scrollWidth=487px on innerWidth=320px → 167px horizontal overflow. The card grid's minmax(320px, 1fr) forces 320px minimum cards, plus padding pushes total beyond viewport.
findings.fix: Use minmax(min(320px, 100%), 1fr) so cards shrink below 320px when needed. Reduce padding to 16px on narrow viewports.

findings.title: Heading hierarchy skips levels (h1 → h3 in workbench)
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users who navigate by headings
findings.location: Workbench section h3 elements directly under document with h1 only
findings.description: Workbench h3 (你的組合 / 適合用在 / 建議補上) appear after the page h1 with no h2 in between (until the categories). Heading navigation (rotor) becomes confusing.
findings.fix: Either add a wrapping section with an h2 (e.g., 'Your Combo Workbench') or change the workbench h3 elements to h2.
findings.code_after:
Combo Workbench

...

你的組合

...

findings.title: <aside> elements are nested inside <section>, not at top level
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users using landmark navigation
findings.description: Per ARIA spec, aside is a complementary landmark and should be top-level. axe flagged this as best-practice violation.
findings.fix: Change <aside class="cat-meta"> to <div> since it's not actually complementary content — it's the heading area of its section. Or restructure so aside is at top level.

findings.title: No skip link to main content; long page (23 cards) requires extensive Tab navigation
findings.description: After fixing keyboard support on cards, this becomes important — without a skip link, users tab through control-bar (7+ stops) before reaching main content.
findings.fix: Add a visually hidden skip link as first focusable element.

findings.title: No structured data (Schema.org JSON-LD), no Open Graph, no canonical URL
findings.affected_users: AI agents, search engines, social media unfurl previews
findings.description: If shared as a personal brand artifact, the lack of metadata means LLM crawlers / search engines can't index it well. No og:title, no canonical, no JSON-LD.
findings.fix: Add basic Open Graph tags + a JSON-LD WebSite/CreativeWork block.

jurisdictions.name: US ADA Title III
jurisdictions.detail: 4 critical Level A violations (keyboard inoperability across primary content), 3 Level AA violations (contrast, reflow). 8,000+ web a11y lawsuits filed annually 2023-2025. Most cite WCAG 1.4.3 + 2.1.1, both flagged here.

jurisdictions.name: Japan JIS X 8341-3:2016
jurisdictions.law: Japanese Industrial Standard for Web Accessibility
jurisdictions.detail: Same WCAG mapping as ADA. Japan emphasizes voluntary compliance for private sites; mandatory for government. Lower litigation risk but reputational risk for organizations engaging APAC public sector.

jurisdictions.name: Taiwan 無障礙網頁規範 2.0
jurisdictions.detail: Based on WCAG 2.0 AA. Same violations apply. Mandatory for government and quasi-public organizations including some NGO classifications.

legal_risk.context_note: This is currently a private brainstorming artifact. Legal exposure becomes real only if shared publicly, embedded in personal brand site, or used by AIA NGO. Keyboard inoperability is the show-stopper for any of those scenarios.

remediation.title: Add keyboard support to cards / combos / synergies / suggestions (4 patterns)
Fix 320px reflow with minmax(min(320px,100%),1fr) + tighter padding
Restructure heading hierarchy (h1 → h2 → h3, no skips)
Replace aside with div for cat-meta blocks
Add Open Graph + JSON-LD if planning to share publicly

testing_recommendations: After fixes, re-run axe-core to confirm 0 violations on color-contrast / region / landmark
Test keyboard-only flow: Tab through atlas, Enter to open detail, Tab in panel, Esc to close
Test with screen reader (NVDA on Windows / VoiceOver on Mac): browse by headings, by landmarks
Real-device test at 320px (iPhone SE) and 768px (iPad portrait)
If publishing: run beacon:inspect again to confirm score >= 85 for shareable artifact
score_interpretation: 70/100 = 'Good — significant issues need attention'. Acceptable for private brainstorming use, NOT acceptable for sharing externally. The 4 keyboard criticals (p0) are show-stoppers — they remove ~30 of the 100 points and are also the easiest wins (~30 min total).
